title: Vendor profile & invoicing handoff — Emberlane
Renewal + capacity upgrade: phased 8→16 GPU commit and throughput SLAs
preview:
From: Sanjay Rao <sanjay@veloxai.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava@redwood.com>
Cc: Leah Kim <leah.kim@veloxai.com>
Date: Tue, 15 Sep 2026 15:10:00 -0700
Subject: Renewal + upgrade — looking to move 8 → 16 GPUs with phased monthly commit

Hi Ava,

Hope you’re well — our renewal with Redwood is coming up (end of month) and the team wants to expand our Dedicated pool. We currently run an 8-GPU reserved pool and would like to commit to 16 GPUs. However, procurement needs a two-month phasing window: start at 12 GPUs month 1–2, then fully scale to 16 for the remainder of the term.

Key asks:
- Updated monthly commit and pricing for 12→16 phased start (12 for first 2 months, then 16)
- Throughput SLOs (tokens/sec or equivalent) and an availability SLA for Dedicated pools
- Any ramp credits or onboarding assistance you can include
- DocuSign/PO flow and procurement contact to finalize order

I attached a short stub PO for reference (Initial_PO_stub.pdf). Can we get a proposal and a call this week?

Thanks,
Sanjay Rao
Head of Platform — VeloxAI
Sanjay@veloxai.com



From: Ava Chen <ava@redwood.com>
To: Sanjay Rao <sanjay@veloxai.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>, Connor O'Brien <connor@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 15 Sep 2026 16:02:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Renewal + upgrade — looking to move 8 → 16 GPUs with phased monthly commit

Hi Sanjay — thanks for the clear note and the PO stub.

Quick summary / next steps from our side:
- We can support a phased ramp (12GPUs for months 1–2, then 16GPUs thereafter) and will reflect that in the order form pricing.
- We offer two SLA tiers for Dedicated: Standard (99.5% availability) and Enterprise (99.9% availability + faster incident response). I’ve looped in Marissa for pricing and Connor for ops/throughput verification.
- Proposed call: Wed 09/16 at 10:00am PT — 30 minutes to review pricing and operational requirements.

I’ll ask Marissa to share a one-sheet with the updated commit numbers and Connor to confirm expected throughput at 16 GPUs given your workload profile.

Best,
Ava Chen
Director, Strategic Accounts
Redwood Inference
ava@redwood.com
Phone: +1 (415) 555-0192



From: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>
To: Sanjay Rao <sanjay@veloxai.com>, Ava Chen <ava@redwood.com>
Cc: Connor O'Brien <connor@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 16 Sep 2026 09:12:00 -07:00
Subject: Proposed commercial terms — VeloxAI renewal & 8→16 expansion (phased)

Hi Sanjay, Ava — attaching the pricing workbook (Velox_Rollout_8to16_Pricing.xlsx). Key points: 

Summary pricing (monthly commit basis):
- Months 1–2 (phased start): 12 GPUs equivalent commit — $XX,XXX / month
- Months 3–12 (full term): 16 GPUs equivalent commit — $YY,YYY / month
- 12-month term, paid monthly. If you move to annual prepay we can increase the discount by 6%.

Ramp credits & onboarding:
- We can provide a one-time ramp credit equal to one week of compute (applied as invoice credit) to offset migration testing costs.
- Dedicated onboarding workshop with Connor + infra team included in the first month.

SLA/throughput guarantees:
- Enterprise SLA (recommended for your latency/uptime needs): 99.9% uptime, 4-hour P1 response, 30-min escalation to engineering on-call.
- Throughput guidance: based on your reported model profile, we project sustained throughput of ~Z tokens/sec per 16-GPU pool under continuous batching; Connor is validating with a sample trace.

Contract & procurement flow:
- We’ll prepare an amendment to your existing order that reflects the phased commit. Once the amendment is signed via DocuSign we’ll schedule the capacity increase and onboarding.

Please confirm whether the phased start (12→16) and the SLA tier above match your expectations. If yes, I’ll generate the DocuSign package and include the commercial redline.

Regards,
Marissa Cole
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
marissa@redwood.com

Attachment: Velox_Rollout_8to16_Pricing.xlsx (pricing workbook, rows: SKU, monthly commit, discount bands, ramp credit line)



From: Sanjay Rao <sanjay@veloxai.com>

Cc: Leah Kim <leah.kim@veloxai.com>
Date: Wed, 16 Sep 2026 09:48:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Proposed commercial terms — VeloxAI renewal & 8→16 expansion (phased)

Thanks Marissa — this looks good and aligned to our internal ask. A few clarifications: 

1) Phasing: procurement wants the formal contract to reflect 12 GPUs for the first two billing cycles, then 16 for the remainder. Can you include the exact dates/billing cycles in the amendment?
2) Throughput: we’ll need Connor’s validated numbers in writing so our SREs can plan capacity mapping. Can that be added as an appendix or Ops note?
3) Legal: please send procurement the DocuSign link and an initial redline so our legal team can start review.

Procurement contact is Leah Kim (cc’d) — she’ll own the PO and signature. Leah, any questions on our side?

If you can get the DocuSign together today, we’ll circulate for internal approval and aim to sign by Friday.

—Sanjay

title: POC acceptance criteria & ops targets — StitchWave trial
preview:
From: Aditya Rao <aditya@redwood.com>
To: Lena Ortiz <lena.ortiz@stitchwave.com>
Cc: Monica Patel <monica@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 15 Sep 2026 14:03:00 -0700
Subject: POC acceptance criteria & ops targets — StitchWave trial

Hi Lena / Tom —

Thanks again for taking the time today — good conversation. Per our call, I wanted to document the proposed POC scope, measurable success criteria, and the short ops runbook so we can align before the kickoff next week. High-level items below; details and the formal acceptance doc attached.

Quick recap (what we agreed):
- Scope: 2 endpoints — summarization for customer support tickets (batch) and routing/classification (real-time).
- Duration: 4-week trial (pilot window), starting week of Sep 21.
- Dataset: StitchWave will provide a scoped sample (up to 10k records) and 200 evaluator prompts + labels for quality checks.

Proposed POC acceptance KPIs (boarding targets; we can adjust):
- Latency: p95 & p99 for the real-time routing endpoint — p95 < 60ms, p99 < 150ms (in-region).
- Throughput: sustained 500 req/s for routing with 100 concurrent users simulated during load windows.
- Cost: target inference cost < $0.0009 / token (hosted Redwood config + quantized model profile).
- Quality: label-based accuracy on the 200 eval prompts — baseline target >= 92% F1 for intent classification; summary ROUGE-L improvement >= 12 over current baseline.

Evaluation plan / owners:
- StitchWave (Lena/Tom): deliver sample dataset + 200 labeled eval prompts by Sep 18.
- Redwood (Aditya / Marcus): provision trial tenant, run canonical workload benchmarks, provide evaluation harness and baseline runs by Sep 24.
- Monica: handle success tracking dashboard and weekly readout cadence with StitchWave.

Operational notes:
- Rollout & fallback policy: requests will default to model A (quantized) with automatic fallback to model B if latency > 250ms or OOM occurs.
- Logging & telemetry: we will provide per-request latency and token-usage traces; StitchWave to confirm HIPAA/GDPR constraints for logs.

Attachments: POC acceptance doc (attached), test prompts CSV (attached).

Next steps (action items):
1) Lena: confirm dataset delivery date (target Sep 18).
2) Marcus: confirm GPU pool sizing and expected throughput test window (I asked for 2 x A100 equivalent for throughput test).
3) Aditya: send final acceptance doc and HubSpot link (sent with this thread).

If that matches your notes, I’ll lock calendar invites for the kickoff (Sep 22) and the first weekly readout (Sep 29).

Thanks —
Aditya
Aditya Rao
Enterprise Sales, Redwood Inference

---
Attachments: StitchWave_POC_Acceptance_v1.pdf (application/pdf), stitchwave-test-prompts.csv (text/csv)

From: Lena Ortiz <lena.ortiz@stitchwave.com>
To: Aditya Rao <aditya@redwood.com>
Cc: Tom Nguyen <t.nguyen@stitchwave.com>, Monica Patel <monica@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 15 Sep 2026 15:12:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: POC acceptance criteria & ops targets — StitchWave trial

Thanks Aditya — this looks great and matches what we discussed. A few clarifications from our side: 

- Dataset: we’ll deliver an initial 5k ticket sample and the 200 evaluator prompts by EOD Sep 17 (internal ops pushed earlier).
- Latency requirement: p99 < 150ms is acceptable, but can we capture p99 over both in-region and cross-region (we have EU users too)? If we can get cross-region p99 < 250ms that would be ideal.
- Cost: StitchWave procurement will want a unit-cost estimate for both hosted and dedicated options — can you include an estimated cost delta if we move to dedicated capacity?

Also — one ask: For the quality metric, rather than ROUGE-L improvement alone, can we include a small human-eval check for 30 random summaries to validate coherence? We’ll own the labeling, but would like Redwood to provide the human-eval rubric.

We’re aligned on the Sep 22 kickoff — I’ll accept once I’ve pushed the dataset and prompts.

Cheers,
Lena
Head of Product, StitchWave

On Tue, Sep 15, 2026 at 14:03, Aditya Rao <aditya@redwood.com> wrote:
> (original message)


From: Marcus Lin <marcus@redwood.com>
To: Aditya Rao <aditya@redwood.com>, Monica Patel <monica@redwood.com>
Cc: Lena Ortiz <lena.ortiz@stitchwave.com>
Date: Wed, 16 Sep 2026 08:18:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: POC acceptance criteria & ops targets — StitchWave trial

Looping in some infra detail here so we don’t miss timelines.

Aditya / Monica — I confirmed capacity for the week-of Sep 21 kickoff: we can provision a single reserved footprint of 2 x A100 equivalent (preemptible pool) for the throughput tests and an autoscaling policy for the live evaluation with an upper cap of 6 GPUs. Notes: 
- For the latency numbers, we’ll run both in-region and EU overlay tests. Expect p95 measurements to be stable; p99 can spike during cold-cache windows. We’ll do warm-up passes prior to any measurement windows.
- Cost modeling: hosted config (quantized) should hit ~ $0.00085/token at the tested batch sizes; dedicated perf (pinned) will be ~10–15% lower per-token but add fixed monthly infra. I’ll draft a quick cost delta and add to the acceptance doc.

I’ll also prepare a short-runbook for timeout/oom conditions and give Marcus infra ownership on the Friday readout.

—Marcus
Senior Solutions Engineer, Redwood Inference


From: Aditya Rao <aditya@redwood.com>
To: Lena Ortiz <lena.ortiz@stitchwave.com>
Cc: Monica Patel <monica@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 16 Sep 2026 09:12:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: POC acceptance criteria & ops targets — StitchWave trial

Perfect — thanks all for the fast turnaround. Updated items and final confirmation below (I’ll attach the versioned acceptance doc to HubSpot after this):

Confirmed changes / final targets:
- Latency: p95 < 60ms (in-region), p99 < 150ms (in-region), cross-region p99 < 250ms (EU).
- Throughput: sustained 500 req/s for routing endpoint during the throughput window; warm-up passes beforehand.
- Cost: target inference cost <= $0.0009/token for hosted quantized profile; expected dedicated delta -10–15% on per-token with monthly reserved fee (Marcus to add $ estimates).
- Quality: automated eval target >= 92% F1 on 200-labeled prompts + human-eval pass on 30 random summaries (rubric attached).

Action items (owners and dates):
- StitchWave: deliver dataset + 200 eval prompts by Sep 17 (Lena).
- Redwood infra: size GPU pool and provide cost delta by Sep 18 (Marcus).
- Redwood eval: run baseline + provide evaluation harness and first-run metrics by Sep 24 (Aditya + Marcus).
- Ops/reporting: weekly readout (Monica) — first session Sep 29.

Kickoff: Sep 22 at 10:00am PT — I’ll send calendar invites (30m kickoff + 60m throughput test session scheduled Sep 24).

If there are no objections I’ll finalize the acceptance doc and push the artifacts to the HubSpot deal record. Thanks!

Aditya
Enterprise Sales, Redwood Inference

---
Attachments pushed to HubSpot deal and Drive: https://hubspot.example.com/deal/4321 | https://drive.google.com/file/d/1a2b3cPOCacceptance

title: Re: DPA — training-use exceptions & international transfer path
preview:
From: Anna Li <anna@clearlinehealth.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>
Cc: procurement@clearlinehealth.com, juris@clearlinehealth.com
Date: Mon, 14 Sep 2026 09:12:00 -0000
Subject: DPA — proposed carve-outs for model training + transfers

Hi Ben / Laura,

Thanks again for the demo last week. Our legal team reviewed the standard DPA and returned a short set of asks that we need Redwood to accept before procurement can sign: primarily two topics — (1) a narrow \"no-training\" carve-out for customer content containing PHI or explicitly-labeled Customer Confidential Data, and (2) an explicit pathway for EU personal data that mandates SCCs or EU-hosted subprocessors.

I've attached the DPA with our proposed redlines (Clearline_DPA_Proposed_redlines.docx). Key asks in brief:
- No training or model improvement on any Customer Content that Customer designates as \"Sensitive Customer Data\" (we provided a definition; sensitive includes PHI, genetic/clinical data, and any data labeled per our internal DLP tagging).
- For telemetry/aggregate usage data, Customer allows use for service improvement only if fully de-identified; need a clause that prohibits re-identification and derivative model training.
- Subprocessor list requirement: 30 days notice + option to object; any transfer outside EEA must be covered by SCCs or explicit EEA hosting.
- Audit rights: annual on-site or remote audit limited to security controls that process Customer Content.

Can Ben or someone on your team review and flag which asks are negotiable vs. require legal escalation? We're hoping to close this week.

Thanks,
Anna
Anna Li
Director, Vendor Risk & Compliance
Clearline Health

Attachment: Clearline_DPA_Proposed_redlines.docx (document with inline comments)

From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Anna Li <anna@clearlinehealth.com>
Cc: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 14 Sep 2026 12:30:00 -0000
Subject: Re: DPA — proposed carve-outs for model training + transfers

Anna — thanks for the clear summary and for attaching the redlines. I've read the highlights and looped in Marissa (legal) to draft language we can live with.

High level reaction from our side:
- We are sympathetic to a strict carve-out for PHI and other high-risk categories; we typically accept a clause that prohibits training on Customer Content that is both (a) marked as \"No-Train / Sensitive\" by the customer and (b) meets a defined sensitive-data schema (PHI, PII, clinical records).
- For telemetry, we prefer the ability to use aggregated, irreversible telemetry for service improvement; we'll propose stronger de-identification guarantees in the DPA.
- Subprocessor workflow: we can provide a rolling list and 30 days notice for additions; for EEA transfers we can either commit to SCCs or perform EEA-only processing for that customer's workloads if they purchase a regional offering.

Marissa — can you propose a compact clause that: (1) defines \"Sensitive Customer Data\" and \"No-Train Data\"; (2) prohibits model weights/parameters being influenced by such data; and (3) specifies remedies if a breach of that clause occurs? Also please advise whether the audit-rights requested are standard or require procurement-level exception.

Anna — if this looks OK I'll get our suggested redlines back to you within 24 hours. If legal needs extra time we'll flag it.

Best,
Ben Carter
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.com | +1 415-555-0192

On Mon, 14 Sep 2026 at 09:12 Anna Li <anna@clearlinehealth.com> wrote:
> Hi Ben / Laura,
>
> Thanks again for the demo last week...

From: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Anna Li <anna@clearlinehealth.com>
Cc: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 15 Sep 2026 08:45:00 -0000
Subject: RE: DPA — proposed carve-outs for model training + transfers (redlines attached)

Hi all,

I've reviewed Clearline's proposed changes and attached our suggested redlines (DPA_redlines_v3.docx). Summary of our position and proposed wording below. I've kept the clauses tight to reduce back-and-forth.

1) Definitions / No-Train carve-out (proposed language snippet):
\"\"No-Train Data\" means Customer Content that Customer has designated in writing as \"No-Train\" or that meets the Sensitive Data Definition (e.g., PHI as defined under applicable law, clinical/genetic data, or data classified as Customer Confidential pursuant to Section X). Redwood shall not use No-Train Data to perform model training, fine-tuning, transfer learning, or otherwise alter model weights or parameters. Redwood may, however, use aggregated, de-identified usage metrics that cannot reasonably be reverse-engineered to reconstruct or re-identify Customer Content for the sole purpose of improving service reliability and scalability.\"\"

2) Subprocessors and transfers:
- We will maintain a public subprocessor list and provide 30 days' notice prior to onboarding new subprocessors.
- For personal data originating in the EEA, Redwood will either process within EEA-hosted infrastructure or apply the EU Standard Contractual Clauses (or equivalent lawful transfer mechanism) prior to onward transfer.

3) Audit & remedies:
- We accept limited audit rights: annual remote audit or one on-site audit per 24 months, scoped to controls that directly process Customer Content. We decline broad forensic access; instead propose evidence packages and SOC2 reports plus a scoped questionnaire.
- Remedies for breach of No-Train clause: immediate cessation of the affected processing, expedited deletion of derived artifacts where feasible, and commercially reasonable remediation steps. We cannot accept unlimited liability for model drift claims; we propose a carve-out limited to direct remediation costs.

Attachments: DPA_redlines_v3.docx (redline track-changes + comment threads).

If these are acceptable in principle, Ben — I can generate the DocuSign-ready clean copy for execution. If Anna needs procurement to escalate a specific clause (e.g., audit frequency), flag which section and we can discuss a one-time exception.

Thanks,
Marissa Cole
Senior Counsel, Redwood Inference
marissa.cole@redwood.com

> On Mon, 14 Sep 2026 at 12:30 Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com> wrote:
> > Anna — thanks for the clear summary...

From: Anna Li <anna@clearlinehealth.com>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Cc: procurement@clearlinehealth.com, juris@clearlinehealth.com
Date: Wed, 16 Sep 2026 10:00:00 -0000
Subject: Re: DPA — proposed carve-outs for model training + transfers

Marissa / Ben — thanks for the quick turnaround and the suggested wording. A few follow-ups from legal/procurement:
- The No-Train definition looks close to what we need. Two asks: add an affirmative obligation for Redwood to permanently delete derived artifacts that can be tied to No-Train Data (where feasible), and add language that prohibits using No-Train Data for synthetic data generation.
- On transfers: we prefer SCCs as the default; if Redwood elects EEA-hosted processing as an alternative, we need a commitment in the SOW and an amendment to the DPA.
- Audit: procurement will accept remote evidence packages and SOC2 plus a one-off right to require a third-party attestation if triggered by an incident.

Can you produce a DocuSign-ready version that includes the No-Train addition and a comment indicating the transfer path options (SCCs or EEA-hosted) so procurement can choose at signature time? If you can send that today, procurement will queue DocuSign.

Best,
Anna Li
Director, Vendor Risk & Compliance
Clearline Health

> On Tue, Sep 15, 2026 at 08:45 Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com> wrote:
> > Hi all,
> > ...

From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Anna Li <anna@clearlinehealth.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 16 Sep 2026 15:20:00 -0000
Subject: Re: DPA — proposed carve-outs for model training + transfers (DocuSign link)

Anna — thanks. Marissa and I updated the No-Train clause to include the prohibition on synthetic-data generation and to add the deletion/remediation language (see attached clean DPA). We've also added an SOW note that gives Clearline the choice at signature: (A) SCCs applied; or (B) EEA-hosted processing to be purchased as an add-on.

I'm initiating the DocuSign envelope now. Execution link: https://docusign.com/CL-2026-DPA
DocuSign notes: please choose the transfer option in the SOW section before signing and route to procurement.

Next steps from my side:
- I'll monitor signature completion and confirm when the executed DPA is in hand.
- Once fully executed, I'll open a ticket with our onboarding team to schedule the regional processing option if you select the EEA-hosted path.

Anna — will you please confirm procurement has the envelope and the expected signer? If procurement wants the SCCs by default we'll mark that in our system.

Thanks,
Ben Carter
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.com | +1 415-555-0192

Attachment: Clearline_DPA_Clean.pdf (DocuSign-ready)

title: Activation thresholds and procurement route confirmation (Novum AI)
preview:
From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>
To: Liam O'Neil <liam.oneil@novumai.com>
Cc: Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwoodinference.com>, Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwoodinference.com>
Date: 2026-09-14T15:02:00-07:00
Subject: Post-call recap — trial success criteria & procurement options

Thanks for the call today, Liam — quick recap and next steps so we stay aligned. Great to hear Novum AI is ready to progress to a 30-day trial focused on embedding and reranking workloads.

Agreed trial-success criteria (tentative):
- Usage threshold: >= 350k tokens/week or 15k embedding requests in a rolling 7-day window.
- Quality bar: reranking NDCG lift >= 6% vs current baseline on the provided eval set.
- Performance: median generation latency < 320ms for 512-token profiles.

Procurement / billing options you asked about:
- PO (preferred by your procurement) — Redwood supports PO + invoice (net30).
- Corporate card — possible for faster activation but may require a card on file from your finance team.
- AWS Marketplace / partner marketplace — we can explore if you want marketplace billing and Central IT approvals; onboarding typically adds 1–3 weeks.

Immediate asks / actions:
1) Liam: share the procurement contact and any PO template or fields Novum requires (line items, GL codes, PO number format).
2) Novum procurement: if PO route, confirm required billing start condition (date vs usage threshold).
3) Redwood (Monica): draft two billing scenarios (billing-on-first-usage-threshold vs scheduled start date) and outline invoice cadence.

Attachments: none for this note — I'll follow up with a short doc that outlines the two activation scenarios and the ops checklist.

Cheers,
Ben Carter
Senior Account Executive | Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwoodinference.com | +1 415-555-0132
redwoodinference.com


From: Liam O'Neil <liam.oneil@novumai.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Novum AI Procurement <procurement@novumai.com>, Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwoodinference.com>
Date: 2026-09-15T09:45:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Post-call recap — trial success criteria & procurement options

Ben — thanks for the clear summary. Quick responses from our side: 

- Procurement contact: Rebecca Clarke (rebecca.clarke@novumai.com). I'll loop her on the PO template and GL code.
- Preferred route: PO + invoice (procurement note: needs an SOW or a short description line and an expected invoice schedule).
- Billing start preference: procurement usually wants a scheduled start date on the invoice (e.g., 01-Oct-2026) rather than an event-triggered billing start, but we can accept event-triggered if Redwood can provide a short credits/offset for the first partial cycle.

I've attached the PO requirements doc from our finance team — it lists fields we must have on invoices and the vendor onboarding checklist. Expect procurement review time ~10 business days after we submit your (vendor) onboarding packet.

Liam
VP Product, Novum AI
liam.oneil@novumai.com | +1 617-555-0897

-----Attachment: Novum_PO_requirements.pdf (pdf, 148KB)-----


From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>
To: Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwoodinference.com>, Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwoodinference.com>
Date: 2026-09-15T14:20:00-07:00
Subject: Internal: Novum — procurement prefers scheduled start; draft options attached

Monica / Evelyn — forwarding Liam's reply. Key points: procurement prefers a scheduled billing start (01-Oct) but will accept event-triggered if we provide first-cycle credit. Procurement review ~10 business days after we submit vendor onboarding.

Can you please: 
- Review attached spreadsheet and propose two concrete options we can present:
  A) Scheduled-start model: invoice dated 2026-10-01, trial activation window 2026-09-16 through 2026-09-30 (no charges until 10/1).
  B) Event-triggered model: billing begins on first day usage crosses the agreed threshold; Redwood issues a pro-rated invoice and we apply a one-time credit equivalent to X% or $Y for the partial cycle.
- Provide operational step list for PO route (vendor onboarding checklist items we need to send to Rebecca).
- Short rec note on implications of Marketplace route (timeline and known controls) — Liam mentioned 1–3 weeks onboarding.

I've attached a quick spreadsheet with suggested credit values and invoice examples. Going to propose a reconciliation cadence (30-day invoice, payment net30) unless finance suggests otherwise.

Thanks — small ask: can we turn this around by EOD tomorrow so I can present both in the call with Liam & Rebecca on Friday?

Ben
-----Attachment: proposed-billing-options.xlsx (xlsx, 12KB)-----

> On Sep 15, 2026, at 09:45, Liam O'Neil <liam.oneil@novumai.com> wrote:
> > Ben — thanks for the clear summary. Quick responses from our side: ...


From: Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>, Liam O'Neil <liam.oneil@novumai.com>
Cc: Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwoodinference.com>
Date: 2026-09-16T08:10:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Activation thresholds and procurement route confirmation (Novum AI)

Thanks, Ben and Liam — quick operational confirmation and next steps from Redwood's side. We can support both the Scheduled-start and Event-triggered billing flows; below are the practical differences and required artifacts.

Option 1 — Scheduled start (recommended if procurement insists):
- Items we need from Novum: completed vendor onboarding packet, PO number, SOW/line-item description (1–2 lines).
- Ops flow: Redwood issues initial invoice dated 2026-10-01 based on committed/est. usage; trial continues through 09/30 with usage reporting. Invoice payment terms: Net30.
- Timeline: once we receive the vendor packet and PO, finance issues invoice within 2 business days. Procurement review (Novum) still applies ~10 business days for onboarding.

Option 2 — Event-triggered (usage threshold):
- On crossing the agreed threshold (we'll instrument and notify via webhook + email), Redwood will generate a pro-rated invoice for the partial cycle and apply the agreed one-time credit.
- Ops flow: vendor packet still required for invoicing setup; if procurement delays onboarding, we can hold invoices but will track balances until onboarding completes.

Marketplace route notes:
- AWS/GCP marketplace billing adds 1–3 weeks for vendor onboarding and revenue share reconciliation; recommended only if Novum prefers centralized cloud billing and there's no rush to start usage.

Success metrics & instrumentation (to avoid billing disputes):
- Daily usage report (tokens & embedding calls) delivered each morning to procurement@novumai.com and liam.oneil@novumai.com.
- Weekly trial health digest (NDCG lift, latency med, error rate).
- Trial success criteria (final): 350k tokens/week OR 15k embeddings/week; NDCG lift >= 6%; median latency < 320ms.

Next steps / owners:
- Novum (Rebecca) — send vendor onboarding packet & PO template by 2026-09-20.
- Ben — present the two billing options on call with Rebecca on 2026-09-17.
- Monica (ops) — prepare vendor packet checklist and invoice samples by 2026-09-16 EOD.
- Redwood Finance — standby for vendor setup once packet received.

I'll attach the vendor checklist template in a follow-up (tracking doc). If Rebecca prefers the scheduled 10/1 invoice, we'll mark that as the primary flow and keep Event-triggered as fallback.

Cheers,
Monica Patel
Revenue Operations Manager | Redwood Inference
monica.patel@redwoodinference.com | +1 415-555-0177

  B) Event-triggered model: billing begins on first day usage crosses the agreed threshold; Redwood issues a pro-rated invoice and we apply a one-time credit equivalent to X% or $Y for the partial cycle.
- Provide operational step list for PO route (vendor onboarding checklist items we need to send to Rebecca).
- Short rec note on implications of Marketplace route (timeline and known controls) — Liam mentioned 1–3 weeks onboarding.

I've attached a quick spreadsheet with suggested credit values and invoice examples. Going to propose a reconciliation cadence (30-day invoice, payment net30) unless finance suggests otherwise.

Thanks — small ask: can we turn this around by EOD tomorrow so I can present both in the call with Liam & Rebecca on Friday?

Ben

> > Ben — thanks for the clear summary. Quick responses from our side: ...


From: Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>, Liam O'Neil <liam.oneil@novumai.com>
Cc: Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwoodinference.com>
Date: 2026-09-16T08:10:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Activation thresholds and procurement route confirmation (Novum AI)

Thanks, Ben and Liam — quick operational confirmation and next steps from Redwood's side. We can support both the Scheduled-start and Event-triggered billing flows; below are the practical differences and required artifacts.

Option 1 — Scheduled start (recommended if procurement insists):
- Items we need from Novum: completed vendor onboarding packet, PO number, SOW/line-item description (1–2 lines).
- Ops flow: Redwood issues initial invoice dated 2026-10-01 based on committed/est. usage; trial continues through 09/30 with usage reporting. Invoice payment terms: Net30.
- Timeline: once we receive the vendor packet and PO, finance issues invoice within 2 business days. Procurement review (Novum) still applies ~10 business days for onboarding.

Option 2 — Event-triggered (usage threshold):
- On crossing the agreed threshold (we'll instrument and notify via webhook + email), Redwood will generate a pro-rated invoice for the partial cycle and apply the agreed one-time credit.
- Ops flow: vendor packet still required for invoicing setup; if procurement delays onboarding, we can hold invoices but will track balances until onboarding completes.

Marketplace route notes:
- AWS/GCP marketplace billing adds 1–3 weeks for vendor onboarding and revenue share reconciliation; recommended only if Novum prefers centralized cloud billing and there's no rush to start usage.

Success metrics & instrumentation (to avoid billing disputes):
- Daily usage report (tokens & embedding calls) delivered each morning to procurement@novumai.com and liam.oneil@novumai.com.
- Weekly trial health digest (NDCG lift, latency med, error rate).
- Trial success criteria (final): 350k tokens/week OR 15k embeddings/week; NDCG lift >= 6%; median latency < 320ms.

Next steps / owners:
- Novum (Rebecca) — send vendor onboarding packet & PO template by 2026-09-20.
- Ben — present the two billing options on call with Rebecca on 2026-09-17.
- Monica (ops) — prepare vendor packet checklist and invoice samples by 2026-09-16 EOD.
- Redwood Finance — standby for vendor setup once packet received.

I'll attach the vendor checklist template in a follow-up (tracking doc). If Rebecca prefers the scheduled 10/1 invoice, we'll mark that as the primary flow and keep Event-triggered as fallback.

Cheers,
Monica Patel
Revenue Operations Manager | Redwood Inference

title: EU go-live + APAC burst schedule negotiation (contract amendment)
preview:
From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.inference>
To: Lena Fischer <lena.fischer@aurorahealth.com>
Cc: Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.inference>
Date: Mon, 14 Sep 2026 08:22:00 -0400
Subject: EU go-live + APAC burst schedule negotiation (contract amendment)

Hi Lena —

Great to align yesterday. Per our call, I wanted to capture the next steps so we can lock the amendment ahead of your October board review. High level: Aurora will sign a one-year amendment to (a) add EU region residency and failover routing, and (b) reserve APAC burst capacity for November–January.

Quick summary / asks:
- EU go-live target: 2026-11-01 (we'll provision staging in EU by 2026-10-10).
- Data residency: primary data stored in our EU tenant (Frankfurt). Cross-region failover will route reads to EU and writes to US only if you enable write-fallback; default configuration is read-only failover.
- APAC burst: reserved pool = 4 x A100 equivalent (estimated 20k inference requests/day peak). Burst window: 2026-11-15 to 2027-01-31.
- SLA ask: 99.5% regional uptime for EU production, with automatic fallback to Hosted (US) under capacity constraints and notification TTL = 15 minutes.

Attachments: aurora-capacity-plan.csv (sample traffic shape) — please confirm traffic percentiles. I attached a draft SOW amendment (will share formal DocuSign link once we align on scope).

Action items for Aurora: please confirm
1) EU go-live date (OK to target 2026-11-01?),
2) whether you require write-fallback for EU failover, and
3) approval to proceed with reserved APAC burst numbers above.

If useful, I can schedule a 30-min technical check with Avery to confirm network / connectivity requirements next week.

Thanks,
Ben Carter
Account Executive | Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.inference | +1 415-555-0192

[Attachment: aurora-capacity-plan.csv (CSV, 38 KB)]

From: Lena Fischer <lena.fischer@aurorahealth.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.inference>
Cc: Tom Reynolds <tom.reynolds@aurorahealth.com>, Aurora Health Systems Procurement <procurement@aurorahealth.com>, Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.inference>
Date: Mon, 14 Sep 2026 13:04:00 +0100
Subject: Re: EU go-live + APAC burst schedule negotiation (contract amendment)

Thanks Ben — good summary. A few clarifications from our side before we move to SOW: 

1) Data residency / failover: our security team is strongly preferring no-write fallback to US during the first 6 months in EU. They will accept read-only failover but want the write-fallback disabled unless we agree a separate control process. Can Redwood support an architecture that disables automated write-fallback and instead uses a manual escalation to open write-fallback under emergency?

2) SLA & audit: 99.5% regional uptime is fine but we will need a clause for incident credit calculation and access to (redacted) incident timelines. Also request SOC2 report and recent penetration test results prior to signature.

3) APAC burst: the traffic profile in your CSV looks reasonable, but procurement needs a fixed committed minimum for pricing. Can you give committed monthly token / request numbers for the reserved pool and a per-request overage rate?

We can confirm target go-live of 2026-11-01 tentatively, pending the above. I've copied Tom (ops) and procurement so they can weigh in on cost and security.

Best,
Lena Fischer
Head of Platform | Aurora Health Systems
lena.fischer@aurorahealth.com

On Mon, Sep 14, 2026 at 08:22 AM Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.inference> wrote:
> Great to align yesterday. Per our call, I wanted to capture the next steps ...


From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.inference>
To: Lena Fischer <lena.fischer@aurorahealth.com>, Tom Reynolds <tom.reynolds@aurorahealth.com>, Aurora Health Systems Procurement <procurement@aurorahealth.com>
Cc: Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.inference>, Avery Johnson <avery.johnson@redwood.inference>
Date: Tue, 15 Sep 2026 09:16:00 -0400
Subject: Re: EU go-live + APAC burst schedule negotiation (contract amendment)

Hi Lena / Tom —

Thanks for the clear requests. Quick answers below and next steps.

1) Write-fallback: Yes — we can support "manual write-fallback." Implementation plan:
   - Default behavior: read-only failover from US -> EU.
   - Manual escalation: Aurora opens a support case and approves write-fallback; Redwood will enable write-fallback for a user-defined window (configurable in console) and log the event.
   - We'll include this in the SOW language and the playbook for on-call ops.

2) SLA & audit package: we'll include incident credit calculation in the amendment (standard formula tied to downtime minutes). I'll share our SOC2 Type II report and the latest pentest summary (redacted) in a secure link. Expect those in a separate email by EOD.

3) APAC burst committed volume & overage pricing: proposed committed minimum = 300k inference units/month (covers baseline + burst capacity), overage = $0.007 per inference unit. I attached a line-item table in the SOW draft. If procurement needs a different structure (e.g., commit in dollars vs units), we can model both.

I've attached the SOW draft (sow-eu-apac-amendment.docx) with the proposed language and the updated capacity CSV. Monica and Avery are looped in to validate network/security and operational steps.

Next steps
- Monica: can you confirm the EU network/ip allowlist steps and timing?
- Avery: propose 30-min technical sync Thurs 09/17 10:00 PT or Fri 09/18 09:00 PT — which works for you, Aurora?
- Procurement: if the committed 300k units/month looks off, flag preferred pricing structure and I'll run a quick model.

Thanks — Ben
Ben Carter
Account Executive | Redwood Inference
[Attachment: sow-eu-apac-amendment.docx (DOCX, 72 KB)]

From: Lena Fischer <lena.fischer@aurorahealth.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.inference>, Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.inference>, Avery Johnson <avery.johnson@redwood.inference>
Cc: Tom Reynolds <tom.reynolds@aurorahealth.com>, Aurora Health Systems Procurement <procurement@aurorahealth.com>
Date: Thu, 16 Sep 2026 11:05:00 -0400
Subject: Re: EU go-live + APAC burst schedule negotiation (contract amendment)

Ben / team — thanks, that addresses most items. Confirmations: 
- EU go-live 2026-11-01 is approved (we'll schedule staging cutover 2026-10-12).
- Manual write-fallback process acceptable — security will sign off on the playbook.
- Procurement will review the 300k units/month commitment and respond by end of day Friday with preferred billing structure.

Avery / Monica: I'll coordinate with your team on the Thurs 10:00 PT slot — that works for our ops lead. Please send the calendar invite.

Once procurement confirms pricing structure, we'll expect a DocuSign link for the amendment and SOW.

Appreciate the quick turnaround.

Best,
Lena

Head of Platform | Aurora Health Systems

title: Hydra AI capacity scenario walkthrough and next steps
preview:
From: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>, Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwoodinference.com>, Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Alex Wu <alex.wu@hydraai.com>
Date: Sat, 12 Sep 2026 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Hydra AI — weekly fleet planning kickoff (scenarios)

Team — starting this thread to align on capacity scenarios for Hydra AI. They closed lead pricing last week and asked for a committed Dedicated pool proposal + burst plan. HubSpot shows a close window in Q4 which could ramp to sustained 2M tokens/day if the pilot goes well.

Key asks from Sales/Finance:
- Provide three capacity scenarios (Conservative, Base, Aggressive) with GPU slot counts, expected latency SLOs, and monthly cost estimate.
- Identify which scenarios require reserved capacity vs burst credits.
- Highlight any lead time risks (hardware, region) and fallback model options.

Logan — can you start with a short sketch of the infrastructure implications? Kimberly/Ben — I'll need a suggested pricing carve for dedicated slots and a short note for procurement cadence.

Laura — thanks for the ping. Quick clarifying notes from our side:
- Pilot target: 200k tokens/day for 6 weeks (evaluation). Production estimate if pilot succeeds: 2M tokens/day steady state by month 3.
- Latency target: p95 <= 500ms for single-turn API usage. Some batch ingest will tolerate higher latency.

From: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwoodinference.com>, Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Alex Wu <alex.wu@hydraai.com>
Date: Tue, 15 Sep 2026 12:18:00 -0700
Subject: RE: Hydra AI — weekly fleet planning kickoff (scenarios)

Ben/Laura — I sketched three infrastructure scenarios in the attached capacity-model-v3.csv (high level):

1) Conservative (pilot-first)
- Dedicated: 2 x A100-80GB slots (reserved for pilot windows), burst available up to +2 slots on demand.
- Expected throughput: ~250k tokens/day sustained across the pool.
- Latency: p95 ~ 350–450ms (depends on batching and prefix cache).
- Lead time: minimal — use existing US-west pool with cold-start allowance. No new hardware procurement.
- Notes: Works for Hydra's pilot. If they exceed pilot, we'd need to transition to Base.

2) Base (target production commit)
- Dedicated: 8 x A100-80GB reserved. Burst: +6 slots (short-term burst credits).
- Expected throughput: ~1.8–2.2M tokens/day depending on prompt length.
- Latency: p95 ~ 300–420ms with optimized batching and KV cache.
- Lead time: 4–6 week provisioning if we need to expand reserved pool beyond current capacity in the region.
- Notes: Balanced cost/latency; recommended baseline for a 12-month commit.

3) Aggressive (fast growth hedge)
- Dedicated: 12 x A100-80GB reserved + 8 x mixed-quantized T4/RTX burst slots for lower-cost inference on background jobs.
- Expected throughput: 3.5–4M tokens/day with cost-optimized routing.
- Latency: p95 ~ 280–380ms for main model; background tasks slower.
- Lead time: up to 8–12 weeks to lock in additional reserved hardware in US-west if we need new racks.
- Notes: Best for accounts pushing multi-region or low-latency SLAs.

Cost roughs (monthly committed GPU cost, ballpark):
- Conservative: $45k/month
- Base: $165k/month (12-month reserved pricing assumed)
- Aggressive: $270k/month

Risks and mitigations:
- Spike risk: If Hydra unexpectedly hits +50% token volume, burst quota may be exhausted — fallback to lower-capacity model variants and tightened batching.
- Warranty/Procurement: New reserved slots need formal finance approval; provisioning timeline depends on available vendor inventory.

I flagged the exact per-slot run-rate in capacity-model-v3.csv. Happy to walk through the math on a call.

— Logan
Logan Wright
Head of Infra Planning
Redwood Inference

title: Clarify: data residency, retention & support access for Acme Health
preview:
From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
To: Sarah Collins <sarah.collins@acmehealth.com>
Cc: Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwood.ai>
Date: Tue, 14 Sep 2026 14:05:00 -0700
Subject: Follow-up: data handling & access controls recap

Hi Sarah — thanks again for the call this morning. Quick recap and a couple of asks so we stay aligned: 

Summary from my side:
- Scope: low-latency inference for patient triage workflows; we will not store raw audio, only transcribed text and derived embeddings.
- Residency: you flagged EU-only storage for any PII-attributable artifacts.
- Retention: default retention target is 7 years for case records; some telemetry may be shorter.
- Support access: need to confirm break-glass procedures, role-based support access, and audit logging requirements.

Asks / next steps:
1) Can you confirm which elements you classify as PII vs. pseudonymous? A short list is fine (SSNs, DOB, patient ID, etc.).
2) Confirm data residency requirement (single country / EU region / specific data centers).
3) If you have a minimal acceptable retention policy (e.g., 7 years) and whether logs must be retained for the same period.
4) We'd like to schedule a technical security follow-up with our security lead Rishi — would Wednesday or Thursday afternoon (CEST) work?

I attached a short template matrix that shows how we typically map roles -> access. If you can drop in any existing controls you already require, we’ll adapt.

Thanks — talk soon.

—
Ben Carter
Enterprise Sales, Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.ai | +1 415-555-0172
[Attachment: initial-access-matrix-template.xlsx (Excel, 18 KB)]

From: Sarah Collins <sarah.collins@acmehealth.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
Cc: Michael Rivera <michael.rivera@acmehealth.com>
Date: Tue, 14 Sep 2026 19:14:00 +0100
Subject: Re: Follow-up: data handling & access controls recap

Ben — thanks for the clear recap. Quick responses inline below and a couple of clarifying Qs.

PII / classification:
- Confirmed PII: patient full name, DOB, national identifier (SSN/NI), contact info, medical record number.
- We plan to send transcribed text and embeddings to Redwood. We consider embeddings pseudonymous unless they map back to MRN.

Residency:
- Requirement: EU-only. Prefer DE or NL regions for storage. No export of PII outside EU without documented authorization.

Retention:
- Case records: 7 years minimum (compliance requirement).
- System logs: 3 years minimum; however, audit logs for support access need 7 years.

Support access / break-glass:
- We require documented break-glass with justification, timestamped approver, and full session recording where possible.
- Emergency access must be limited to named vendor support roles and logged to an immutable audit trail.

Questions:
- Can Redwood provide a signed DPA that specifies EU processing and subprocessors?
- What level of session recording do you capture for support (screens, commands, ephemeral tokens)?

I can do Thu 16:00 CEST for the tech call. I attached our top-level data-classification PDF for context.

Best,
Sarah
Sarah Collins
Head of Compliance, Acme Health
sarah.collins@acmehealth.com
[Attachment: acme-data-classification.pdf (PDF, 72 KB)]

From: Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwood.ai>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>, Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwood.ai>
Cc: Sarah Collins <sarah.collins@acmehealth.com>, Michael Rivera <michael.rivera@acmehealth.com>
Date: Wed, 15 Sep 2026 08:02:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Fwd: Re: Follow-up: data handling & access controls recap

Ben / Lauren — thanks for looping me in. Summary of what we can offer today and gaps to highlight for Acme: 

1) Data residency
- We currently support EU region pinning to both AWS eu-central-1 (Frankfurt) and GCP europe-west4 (Netherlands). We can commit to EU-only processing in contract (DPA) and exclude cross-border exports without documented consent.

2) Retention and logs
- Case data retention: configurable per-customer; we can set a 7-year retention TTL on persisted artifacts (S3/GCS lifecycle + DB TTL).
- Audit logs (support access records): we retain structured logs for 7 years behind WORM-backed storage (immutable logs) as an add-on. Default system telemetry is 3 years.

3) RBAC / least privilege
- Roles we provide: Owner (customer admin), Admin (config + user mgmt), Support Engineer (diagnostic access), Read-only Auditor.
- Support Engineer role is time-bound and scoped to namespaces/jobs. We can implement just-in-time (JIT) elevation for specific actions.

4) Break-glass / emergency access
- Flow: Support opens a break-glass ticket in our system; customer approver must acknowledge within 1 hour for full privileged session. If approver is unavailable, support access can be granted under emergency policy but is marked "Emergency" and triggers an immediate write-once audit log + session recording.
- Telemetry captured: session start/end, user, requester, justification, commands run, API calls made, keys used. Screens / session video recording is available for dedicated customers who purchase session-recording addon (privacy note: recordings are stored in WORM-backed storage and encrypted).

5) Evidence / contract items
- We can provide a DPA and current subprocessors list; SOC2 report is available under NDA.

Attachments included: access-matrix.xlsx (role mapping draft), breakglass-procedures.docx (procedure + audit template). These are drafts to bring to the call.

Proposed agenda for Thu 16:00 CEST call:
- 10 min: quick architecture pass (data flow, residency)
- 15 min: access controls & break-glass demo (Rishi)
- 15 min: logging & retention options 
- 10 min: contract evidence & next steps

If that looks good I will join Thu 16:00 CEST.

—
Rishi Malhotra
Security & Applied ML Ops, Redwood Inference
rishi.malhotra@redwood.ai
[Attachment: access-matrix.xlsx (Excel, 24 KB)]
[Attachment: breakglass-procedures.docx (Word, 36 KB)]

> 
> Questions:

> - What level of session recording do you capture for support (screens, commands, ephemeral tokens)?
>

From: Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwood.ai>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>, Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwood.ai>

Subject: Re: Fwd: Re: Follow-up: data handling & access controls recap

Ben / Lauren — thanks for looping me in. Summary of what we can offer today and gaps to highlight for Acme: 

1) Data residency
- We currently support EU region pinning to both AWS eu-central-1 (Frankfurt) and GCP europe-west4 (Netherlands). We can commit to EU-only processing in contract (DPA) and exclude cross-border exports without documented consent.

2) Retention and logs
- Case data retention: configurable per-customer; we can set a 7-year retention TTL on persisted artifacts (S3/GCS lifecycle + DB TTL).
- Audit logs (support access records): we retain structured logs for 7 years behind WORM-backed storage (immutable logs) as an add-on. Default system telemetry is 3 years.

3) RBAC / least privilege
- Roles we provide: Owner (customer admin), Admin (config + user mgmt), Support Engineer (diagnostic access), Read-only Auditor.
- Support Engineer role is time-bound and scoped to namespaces/jobs. We can implement just-in-time (JIT) elevation for specific actions.

4) Break-glass / emergency access
- Flow: Support opens a break-glass ticket in our system; customer approver must acknowledge within 1 hour for full privileged session. If approver is unavailable, support access can be granted under emergency policy but is marked "Emergency" and triggers an immediate write-once audit log + session recording.
- Telemetry captured: session start/end, user, requester, justification, commands run, API calls made, keys used. Screens / session video recording is available for dedicated customers who purchase session-recording addon (privacy note: recordings are stored in WORM-backed storage and encrypted).

5) Evidence / contract items
- We can provide a DPA and current subprocessors list; SOC2 report is available under NDA.

Attachments included: access-matrix.xlsx (role mapping draft), breakglass-procedures.docx (procedure + audit template). These are drafts to bring to the call.

title: Asteria Health - Dedicated capacity & SKU proposal (H100 / A100 / L4)
preview:
From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>
To: Samantha Wu <samantha.wu@asteriahealth.com>
Cc: Irene Choi <irene.choi@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Mon, 14 Sep 2026 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Dedicated capacity sizing & SKU options — quick ask

Hi Samantha —

Great speaking yesterday. Per our discussion I pulled together an operational framing so we can pick the right Dedicated SKU mix (H100 / A100 / L4) for Asteria's inference footprint. Quick background questions that will help finalize the sizing: 

- Typical request latency SLO (p95) for real-time patient inference?
- Median and 95th percentile sequence length and tokens per request (examples OK)
- Percentage of long-context workloads (>8k tokens) vs short (<=2k)
- Any strict memory residency requirements (model state retention, KV cache expectations)?
- Monthly committed QPS or token budget (if already estimated)

Attached I included a couple of sample worksheets — one rough cost/throughput matrix and a blank mutual action plan worksheet. If you can share a small sample of production traces or the attached CSV filled with token distribution, we can map a recommended SKU mix and reserve/burst band.

Operationally, our typical guidance:
- H100: best for very large-context or memory-bound models where single-GPU memory and NVLink matters; higher per-hour cost but fewer nodes for memory-heavy workloads.
- A100: strong general-purpose option for throughput at a lower unit price than H100; good fit when model size fits within 40GB / 80GB variants and you want predictable throughput.
- L4: cost-efficient for short-context, latency-sensitive workloads and embedding-heavy pipelines; lower memory but high inference efficiency for smaller models and quantized weights.

Action: If you can paste a short telemetry sample by EOD Wed, I’ll run a quick slotting run and return a proposed Dedicated mix with reserve vs burst ratios and expected p95 latency impact.

Thanks —
Ben
Senior Solutions Engineer
Redwood Inference

Attachments: asteria_workload_samples.csv (csv), RDW-dedicated-sku-estimate-v1.xlsx (spreadsheet)

From: Samantha Wu <samantha.wu@asteriahealth.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Mon, 14 Sep 2026 10:03:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Dedicated capacity sizing & SKU options — quick ask

Ben —

Thanks for the clear note. I attached a small sample trace and a rough QPS expectation from our alpha. High-level answers:

- p95 SLO target: 250ms for clinical routing calls; bulk batch jobs can be 2–3s.
- Token distribution (attached CSV): median ~480 tokens, 95th ~4,200 tokens, long tail to 12k (~2% of traffic).
- Long-context workload (12k) is mostly for multi-document synthesis in nightly batch; real-time calls are typically <2k.
- KV/state: we rely on a small per-session KV for conversation context; not large, but we prefer not to spill to disk during normal inference.
- Committed target: starting at 3k QPS with bursts to 6k during business hours; monthly token budget TBD but rough estimate ~1.8B tokens/month.

We can share the sample traces as a CSV — attached. Happy to hop on a short call if helpful.

Best,
Samantha Wu
Head of Product
Asteria Health

Attachments: asteria_workload_samples.csv (csv)

From: Irene Choi <irene.choi@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Mon, 14 Sep 2026 12:45:00 -0700
Subject: Fwd: Asteria traces — initial SKU mapping thoughts

Ben / Marcus —

Forwarding Samantha’s trace. Initial read: majority short requests (~480 tokens) and a small long-tail at ~12k (2%). Their p95 latency target 250ms is tight, and they plan to ramp to steady 3k QPS. Points to consider before we propose:

- Memory residency: "prefer not to spill to disk" — we should design for KV in-GPU cache for session state. That biases toward H100/A100 based on model sizes.
- Mixed workload: latency-sensitive short requests + occasional long-context jobs. H100 gives headroom for long-context batching but at higher cost. L4 could cover 70–80% of short requests for cost savings if we accept small p95 degradation.
- Autoscaling & fallbacks: propose a primary L4 pool for short-context traffic with automatic fallthrough to A100 in the event of L4 slot saturation, and a small H100 pool reserved for long-context batch and ad-hoc high-memory fallbacks.

I sketched a reserve/burst split in the attached spreadsheet — Marcus, can you sanity-check memory footprint assumptions and quantization paths?

Irene
Director, Solutions Architecture
Redwood Inference

Attachments: RDW-dedicated-sku-estimate-v1.xlsx (spreadsheet), SAR-telemetry-snippets.txt (text)

From: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwoodinference.com>
To: Irene Choi <irene.choi@redwoodinference.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Mon, 14 Sep 2026 15:20:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Fwd: Asteria traces — initial SKU mapping thoughts

Irene —

Checked the samples. Quick technical notes:

- Model sizing: assume their production model variants are 30–40B parameter family (based on token patterns). That maps to ~40–80GB unquantized; we should expect to rely on 80GB A100s or H100s if they want full fp16 without sharding.
- Quantization: if Asteria is willing to accept 4-bit quantization for shorter-context models, L4 becomes very attractive; we can run embeddings + short generation on L4 with quantized weights for ~2–3x cost improvement.
- KV cache: built GPU-resident KV for session context is feasible on 80GB-class GPUs; 40GB will need more aggressive eviction or prefix caching.
- Concurrency/tiling: to meet 3k QPS steady-state with median 480 tokens, an L4 pool sized for throughput is cost-efficient. Reserve H100 slots at ~5–7% of capacity strictly for long-context batch and fallback. A100 (80GB) can be the medium tier for mixed or mid-size models.

Ops notes: we should recommend a staged rollout. Phase 1 (Q1) — reserve A100 80GB pool for baseline and an L4 pool at 50% of expected short traffic; Phase 2 (Q2) — ramp L4 to 70–80% coverage with A100 as fallback; keep H100 minimal reserved for long-context and emergency.

I updated the workbook with memory footprints and a sample reserve/burst calculator.

—Marcus
Principal Inference Engineer
Redwood Inference

Attachments: RDW-dedicated-sku-estimate-v1.xlsx (updated)

From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>
To: Samantha Wu <samantha.wu@asteriahealth.com>, procure@asteriahealth.com
Cc: Irene Choi <irene.choi@redwoodinference.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, 15 Sep 2026 08:30:00 -0700
Subject: Proposal: hybrid Dedicated pools (L4 primary, A100 fallback, H100 reserved)

Hi Samantha —

Thanks again for the telemetry. Based on the traces and internal sizing review, here’s a pragmatic proposal we can take to contract: 

Summary recommendation (high-level)
- Primary pool: L4 (quantized models) sizing to cover ~70% of steady short-context QPS (cost-efficient for median 480-token requests).
- Fallback pool: A100 80GB to cover remaining short-to-mid workloads and handle L4 saturation without p95 regression.
- Reserved H100: a small pool (5–7% of peak capacity) for long-context runs and memory-bound fallbacks (nightly multi-document synthesis).

Reserve vs burst (example numbers)
- Reserve: L4 150 slots, A100 40 slots, H100 4 slots (baseline committed).
- Burst capacity: additional 80 L4 slots + 30 A100 slots available via burst pool; H100 burst on demand (pre-warmed) but billed only when active.

Expected outcomes- Meets p95 250ms for short-context when L4+fallback configured and autoscaling thresholds tuned.
- Keeps committed spend lower by offloading bulk short traffic to L4 while preserving latency via A100 fallback.
- H100 reserved for long-context ensures night-batch SLAs without overcommitting expensive capacity.

Next steps
1) Approve the reserve/burst example numbers (or provide preferred committed slot counts).
2) Share final token budget to finalize monthly pricing estimate.
3) Sign the attached mutual action plan; we’ll schedule a 45-minute run-through with Marcus and our Ops team to finalize autoscale fencing and pre-warm policies.

I attached a filled version of the estimate workbook with the slot math and cost delta. Happy to adjust numbers on a quick call.

Best,
Ben Carter
Senior Solutions Engineer
Redwood Inference

Attachments: RDW-dedicated-sku-estimate-v1.xlsx (proposal), asteria_workload_samples.csv (reference)

title: Model shortlist & throughput expectations — onboarding kickoff
preview:
From: Olivia Barnes <olivia@clariohealth.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
Cc: Samir Patel <samir@clariohealth.com>
Date: Sun, 13 Sep 2026 20:12:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Intro — Model shortlist + sizing questions for onboarding

Thanks for the quick intro, Ben.

We’re kicking off implementation next week and want to align on the model choices and the token/throughput sizing assumptions before the kickoff call. High level: our product will send a mix of short clinical extraction prompts (~200–400 tokens) and long-context summarization requests (up to 6,000 tokens) when customers upload multi-page PDFs. Estimated QPS is small to start (~0.2 reqs/sec) but bursts to ~6 reqs/sec during business hours for certain clinics.

Attached: clario_context_samples.json (3 anonymized payloads).

Ask: can Redwood provide a recommended shortlist (including lower-cost quantized variants) and a rough per-model throughput and token-cost estimate for the two workload classes (short extraction vs long summarization)? Also: any constraints we should plan for regarding context length and KV cache behavior for 6k-token inputs?

Thanks — Olivia
Olivia Barnes
Head of Product, Clario Health


From: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
To: Olivia Barnes <olivia@clariohealth.com>
Cc: Samir Patel <samir@clariohealth.com>, Connor O'Brien <connor@redwood.com>, Mina Haddad <mina@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 14 Sep 2026 09:03:12 -0700
Subject: Re: Intro — Model shortlist + sizing questions for onboarding

Hi Olivia / Samir —

Thanks — I reviewed the samples briefly and shared them with our modeling + infra folks. High level answers up front:

- Shortlist (fast/cheaper path): Llama-3-16k quant-8 (good for extraction), Mosaic-7B-int8 for super low cost testing.
- Shortlist (long-context/high-quality): Llama-3-70b-32k float16, and a quantized 70B-32k variant (we can fall back to 16k if you want lower cost).

Estimated token profile and throughput (very coarse ballpark):
- Extraction (200–400 tokens): 80–120 tokens/sec per GPU for 16k-quant models; ~8–12 requests/sec per GPU depending on batchability.
- Long summarization (6k input + 400 summary = ~6400 tokens): for 70B-32k float16 expect ~0.4–0.8 reqs/sec per GPU (long sequences are expensive); quantized 70B will be 1.5–2x slower/higher latency but lower memory pressure.

I’m looping Connor and Mina to give a quick throughput/cost sketch and a proposed sizing worksheet. Can we schedule a 30-min sizing sync tomorrow? I’ll include a small mutual action plan.

Best,
Ben
Ben Carter
Senior Sales Engineer, Redwood Inference


From: Connor O'Brien <connor@redwood.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>, Mina Haddad <mina@redwood.com>, Olivia Barnes <olivia@clariohealth.com>
Cc: Samir Patel <samir@clariohealth.com>
Date: Mon, 14 Sep 2026 10:25:44 -0700
Subject: Re: Intro — Model shortlist + sizing questions for onboarding

Quick internal->external note and internal calc:

Olivia — copying simplified numbers so you can validate:

1) Extraction workload (200–400 tokens)
- Typical token generation per request: 150 tokens average.
- Model: Llama-3-16k quant-8 -> measured throughput roughly 90–130 tok/s per GPU for generation-only workloads; expect ~10 reqs/sec/GPU if payloads are small and batching occurs.
- Cost estimate (hosted pricing ballpark): $0.002–0.004 per 1k input+output tokens (varies by reserved vs hosted).

2) Long summarization (6k tokens in, 400 out)
- End-to-end token count ~6400.
- Llama-3-70b-32k float16 -> sustained generation throughput ~100–200 tok/s on high-memory A100-class instances; real reqs/sec ~0.02–0.1 reqs/sec per GPU for single-request long runs. With batching and prefix caching (if you repeatedly summarize overlapping pages) you can amortize some cost.
- Quant-70B variants can reduce memory footprint but increase latency per token; good for capacity-constrained dedicated pools.

Operational notes:
- For 6k context, be explicit about tokenization differences (PDF -> extracted text often has extra tokens in boilerplate). Send us a couple more real payloads with OCR artifacts so we can run a tokenizer pass.
- KV cache: long single-shot summarization doesn't benefit from KV cache unless you split work into windowed prompts. If you plan to do multi-step reasoning across the same document, we can preserve KV cache between steps for faster re-runs.

I attached a preliminary sizing worksheet (redwood_sizing_sheet_v1.xlsx) with three scenarios (pilot, steady-state, peak day). Ben — linking that to the MAP in HS record HS-78342.

—Connor
Connor O'Brien
Principal Solutions Architect, Redwood Inference


From: Mina Haddad <mina@redwood.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>, Connor O'Brien <connor@redwood.com>, Olivia Barnes <olivia@clariohealth.com>
Date: Mon, 14 Sep 2026 12:18:06 -0700
Subject: Re: Intro — Model shortlist + sizing questions for onboarding

Added concrete numbers for the pilot (assume 30-day pilot):
- Pilot traffic: 0.2 reqs/sec average -> ~17k requests/day -> approximate tokens/day (mix): 17k * 600 tokens avg = ~10.2M tokens/day.
- Recommended pilot capacity: 2x 16k-quant GPUs (shared pool) for extra headroom + 1x 70B-32k float16 on dedicate for nightly long summarization jobs, or use burst to hosted for long runs.
- Estimated pilot monthly token cost (hosted equivalent): $3.2k–$6k (depends on exact model choice / quantization).

Notes:
- Autoscaling: configure up-down cooldowns conservatively for long-run jobs to prevent thrash.
- Fallback policy: route long summarization to a compatible 16k-quant fallback if 32k instances unavailable; add warning in logs when truncation occurs.

Attachment: redwood_sizing_sheet_v1.xlsx (tabbed scenarios + breakouts). I also uploaded the workbook to the Drive link.

—Mina
Mina Haddad
Inference Optimization Lead, Redwood Inference


From: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
To: Olivia Barnes <olivia@clariohealth.com>
Cc: Connor O'Brien <connor@redwood.com>, Mina Haddad <mina@redwood.com>, Samir Patel <samir@clariohealth.com>
Date: Mon, 14 Sep 2026 18:45:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Intro — Model shortlist + sizing questions for onboarding

Olivia —

This looks like a good starting plan. Next steps I propose:
1) You confirm two representative OCR output files (if possible, include a noisy and a clean example) so we can run tokenizer + memory estimates.
2) We book a 30-min sizing call tomorrow (I’ll send calendar invite).
3) I’ll open a MAP in HubSpot (HS-78342) with milestone: pilot capacity validation (target: 2026-09-24).

If that works I’ll also share a short runbook documenting fallback behavior and an expected cost range for both pilot and steady-state.

Thanks again — excited to get Clario onboarded.

Ben
Ben Carter
Senior Sales Engineer, Redwood Inference
Attachments: redwood_sizing_sheet_v1.xlsx (see Drive link)

title: Request: one-time onboarding credits for SprigTech POC (exec clearance)
preview:
From: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.com>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.com>, Chloe Martin <chloe_martin@redwood.com>
Cc: Rishi Malhotra <rishi_malhotra@redwood.com>
Date: Sat, 12 Sep 2026 09:14:00 -0700
Subject: Request: one-time onboarding credits for SprigTech POC (exec clearance)

Team —

Quick ask for executive clearance. SprigTech (Liz Chen) completed a very positive technical spike last week against the hosted runtime; product feedback was excellent and they’ve agreed to a deeper POC for their onboarding flow. They hit a billing/PLG limit when the integration began firing at scale — their procurement is slow and they can’t finalize a PO until late October. If we don’t give them runway they’ll pause the POC and likely push their rollout to H2 2027.

What I’m requesting: one-time onboarding credit of 60,000 token-equivalent credits (valid for 30 days) and an extension of the POC window by 30 days beyond our standard PLG limits. This is a carve for a strategic mid-market account that promises a multi-cluster Dedicated discussion next quarter; the HS opportunity is forecasted at $420k ARR.

Rationale in short:
- Technical success: latency and safety gates met in small-scale tests (see attached evaluation signals).
- Momentum: customer engineering is already integrated and they’re running regression tests — losing momentum is high risk.
- Procurement timing: legal/procurement signoff delayed by internal purchasing cycles at SprigTech; they’ve asked for a temporary extension to finish diligence.

Asks:
1) Marissa — can you confirm deal economics and whether we should attach a conditional clause (e.g., credits expire earlier if PO is not received)?
2) Chloe — legal check on a short T&Cs addendum to cover single-use credit allocation.
3) Rishi — executive OK (one-time) or alternatively discretionary partial uplift + extended evaluation?

Attaching: sprigtech-eval-signals.pdf (high-level usage and feature hits). HubSpot record linked below.

Thanks — Ben
Ben Carter
Enterprise Sales 
Redwood Inference


From: Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.com>
Cc: Chloe Martin <chloe_martin@redwood.com>, Rishi Malhotra <rishi_malhotra@redwood.com>
Date: Sat, 12 Sep 2026 11:03:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Request: one-time onboarding credits for SprigTech POC (exec clearance)

Thanks Ben — quick clarifying Qs before I loop in finance/deal desk:
- Please justify why 60k vs 40k. What does that buy the customer (tokens by milestone)?
- What’s the conversion probability with and without the uplift (close plan)?
- Any precedent (comparable credits) we’ve granted last quarter for mid-market?

If you can attach an abbreviated usage export and a 1-line consumer-impact metric (e.g., pages per session, model calls/day), I’ll run this to Deal Desk. No objection in principle to a conditional clause.

Marissa
VP Sales Ops


From: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.com>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.com>, Chloe Martin <chloe_martin@redwood.com>
Cc: Rishi Malhotra <rishi_malhotra@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 14 Sep 2026 09:22:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Request: one-time onboarding credits for SprigTech POC (exec clearance)

Marissa — attaching the usage extract (sprigtech-usage-week1-3.csv). Quick answers inline: 
- Why 60k: first 20k covers initial onboarding smoke tests (already consumed), next 40k is necessary to run the full regression suite across 3 geographies they test for latency variance. If we only give 40k they will re-scope and push critical E2E tests out.
- Conversion probability: with uplift and extension I’m modeling a 60% chance to convert to paid Dedicated talks within 90 days; without uplift we fall to ~25% and they likely table the project.
- Precedent: Q2 we granted a 40k one-off to a comparable mid-market account during procurement delay; that converted in 45 days.

Customer note: Liz (cc’d from previous threads) confirmed procurement will sign the PO contingent on final security review; they asked specifically for a short runway extension to finish SSO and KMS integration.

If Deal Desk prefers less credits + a short discount on committed spend, I can propose 30k credits + 6% first-year discount. Otherwise the full 60k keeps the technical momentum.

Attachments: sprigtech-usage-week1-3.csv
Related: https://app.hubspot.com/contacts/12345/company/67890

Ben


From: Rishi Malhotra <rishi_malhotra@redwood.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.com>
Cc: Chloe Martin <chloe_martin@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 14 Sep 2026 16:38:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Request: one-time onboarding credits for SprigTech POC (exec clearance)

Ben / team — thank you for the thorough notes. I’ll approve a one-time, conditional onboarding credit allocation. Terms below: 

- Approval: 50,000 token-equivalent credits, valid for 30 days from issuance.
- Condition: Credits granted only if SprigTech signs the standard POC addendum acknowledging credits are non-renewable and will be credited against the first invoice if a PO is executed within 90 days.
- Escalations: Chloe, please draft a short addendum and confirm there are no unusual indemnities or IP exposures. Deal Desk (Marissa) to confirm accounting treatment and that we record as contra-revenue per policy.
- Alternative if procurement stalls beyond 30 days: deal desk to evaluate 1-time extension of an additional 15k credits contingent on a signed procurement milestone and a named exec sponsor at SprigTech.

Ben — please communicate the conditional approval to Liz and procurement and send me a one-line win probability update after the addendum is accepted.

Rishi
Chief Revenue Officer
Redwood Inference

title: Vendor onboarding: invoice channel selection and envelope scenarios
preview:
From: finance@lumos-supply.com (Lumos AP)
To: aisha.rahman@redwood.ai
Cc: 
Date: Sat, 12 Sep 2026 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Invoice submission options + sample file for multi-PO envelopes

Hi Aisha,

Thanks for the onboarding call earlier. We want to finalize how to send invoices to Redwood once our account is activated. Two quick questions so we can update our AP playbook: 

1) Can you confirm whether Redwood prefers invoices via the vendor portal (SFTP/portal upload) or an email fallback? If email is acceptable, is there a required subject token or header we should include?

2) We often send a single email with multiple PDFs (one per PO) and also sometimes a CSV envelope mapping when there are many line items across POs. Attached is an anonymized sample CSV to show our typical envelope. Can your team confirm required column names for mapping PO -> invoice -> line items?

Attachments: lumos_sample_invoice_envelope.csv (CSV), lumos_vendor_onboarding_form.pdf (PDF)

Appreciate a short checklist we can follow.

Best,
Finance Team
Lumos Supply
finance@lumos-supply.com




From: aisha.rahman@redwood.ai
To: finance@lumos-supply.com (Lumos AP)
Cc: monica.patel@redwood.ai, vanessa.ortiz@redwood.ai
Date: Sat, 12 Sep 2026 11:03:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Invoice submission options + sample file for multi-PO envelopes

Hi Lumos team — thanks for the clear samples. A few notes so we can align on the onboarding path: 

Summary recommendation (short): portal-first; email as a fallback for edge cases and PO-less invoices. 

Checklist (short):
- Portal preferred: upload single-invoice PDF per file; system auto-maps PO if PO# present in header or CSV mapping.
- Email fallback: subject token MUST contain: "Inv-Redwood:<vendor-id>" (we will provide vendor-id on activation).
- CSV envelope: required columns we need: vendor_id, invoice_number, invoice_date (YYYY-MM-DD), total_amount (USD), po_number (optional), line_item_sku, line_item_qty, line_item_unit_price. If PO-less, put po_number blank and set invoice_type=credit_or_debit accordingly.

I attached the onboarding form you sent, plus a one-page example we use for multi-PO envelopes. Can you confirm Lumos can populate the "invoice_type" and "vendor_id" columns? If not, we can accept a wrapper file naming pattern: lumos_<vendor-id>_envelope_YYYYMMDD.csv in the subject.

Attachments: lumos_vendor_onboarding_form.pdf (returned with small edits), lumos_sample_invoice_envelope.csv (annotated)

Thanks — cc'ing Monica (onboarding) and Vanessa (billing ops) to pick up mapping details and SLA.

Best,
Aisha Rahman
Manager, Procurement Ops
Redwood Inference
aisha.rahman@redwood.ai
